sections.heading: Recent childhood mortality rates were lower than in 2022
sections.body: For the 0-4 years before the survey, every listed childhood mortality rate was lower in 2025 than in 2022. Under-5 mortality fell from 26 to 22 deaths per 1,000 live births, while infant mortality fell from 22 to 18. Neonatal mortality moved from 15 to 12, post-neonatal mortality from 7 to 6, and child mortality from 5 to 3. The pattern is modest but broad across the indicators rather than concentrated in only one age interval.

takeaways: In the 0-4 years before the survey, under-5 mortality was 22 deaths per 1,000 live births in 2025, down from 26 in 2022.
Infant mortality also declined, from 22 to 18 deaths per 1,000 live births.
The 2025 rates were lower than 2022 across neonatal, post-neonatal, infant, child, and under-5 mortality.
